TypeSignature.Value: .class public auto ansi abstract beforefieldinit EventInfo extends System.Reflection.MemberInfo
Public MustInherit Class EventInfo Inherits MemberInfo
public ref class EventInfo abstract : System::Reflection::MemberInfo

Docs.summary: Discovers the attributes of an event and provides access to event metadata.

remarks.format:
## Remarks Use the <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo> class to inspect events and to hook up event handlers, as shown in the example code for the <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo.AddEventHandler*> method.
> [!NOTE] > <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo> is not intended to be used to raise events. An object raises events as dictated by its internal state.
Events are used with delegates. An event listener instantiates an event-handler delegate that is invoked whenever the event is raised by an event source. In order to connect to the event source, the event listener adds this delegate to the invocation list on the source. When the event is raised, the invoke method of the event-handler delegate is called. Both multicast and single-cast event notifications are supported. The `Add` and `Remove` methods, as well as the event-handler delegate class associated with an event, must be marked in the metadata.
Delegates are object-oriented function pointers. In C or C++, a function pointer is a reference to a method. In contrast to the C or C++ function pointer, a delegate contains two references: a reference to a method and a reference to an object that supports the method. Delegates can invoke a method without knowing the class type that declares or inherits the method. Delegates need only know the return type and parameter list of the method.
The event model works equally well for single-cast and multicast delegates. When the delegate's invoke method is called, only a single object will have a method called on it. A multicast modifier can be applied to a delegate declaration, which allows multiple methods to be called when the invoke method of the delegate is called.
Calling <xref:System.Reflection.ICustomAttributeProvider.GetCustomAttributes*?displayProperty=nameWithType> on `EventInfo` when the `inherit` parameter of `GetCustomAttributes` is `true` does not walk the type hierarchy. Use <xref:System.Attribute?displayProperty=nameWithType> to inherit custom attributes.
## Examples The following code gets an <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo> object for the <xref:System.Windows.Forms.Control.Click> event of the <xref:System.Windows.Forms.Button> class.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System/Type/GetEvent/type_getevent1.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System/Type/GetEvent/type_getevent1.vb" id="Snippet1":::

block.para: When you inherit from , you must override the following members: , , and .

MemberSignature.Value: .method familyhidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor() cil managed

Docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

MemberSignature.Value: public virtual void AddEventHandler (object? target, Delegate? handler);
.method public hidebysig newslot virtual instance void AddEventHandler(object target, class System.Delegate handler) cil managed
Public Overridable Sub AddEventHandler (target As Object, handler As Delegate)
abstract member AddEventHandler : obj * Delegate -> unit override this.AddEventHandler : obj * Delegate -> unit
public: virtual void AddEventHandler(System::Object ^ target, Delegate ^ handler);
public virtual void AddEventHandler (object target, Delegate handler);

Docs.param: Encapsulates a method or methods to be invoked when the event is raised by the target.
Docs.summary: Adds an event handler to an event source.

remarks.format:
## Remarks This method attempts to add a delegate to synchronize the event on the target object.
Each time the event is raised by the target parameter, the method or methods encapsulated by the handler are invoked.
You might use the `AddEventHandler` method when you load a type after the program is already compiled, when it is not possible to use the C# += syntax or the Visual Basic `WithEvents` and `Handles` keywords to hook up the event.
## Examples The following example creates an instance of the <xref:System.Timers.Timer?displayProperty=nameWithType> class, creates an event handler using a dynamic assembly, and hooks up the dynamic event handler. All actions are performed using late binding.
The <xref:System.Timers.Timer> instance is stored in a variable of type <xref:System.Object>, and all code that accesses the <xref:System.Timers.Timer> does so late-bound. The example uses the <xref:System.Type.GetEvent*?displayProperty=nameWithType> method to get the <xref:System.Timers.Timer.Elapsed> event, and the <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo.EventHandlerType> property to get the delegate type for the event.
The example gets a <xref:System.Reflection.MethodInfo> for the `Invoke` method of the delegate type and obtains the signature of the delegate from the <xref:System.Reflection.MethodInfo> instance. The example then creates a dynamic assembly with one module containing a single type named `Handler` and gives the type a `static` method (`Shared` method in Visual Basic) named `DynamicHandler` that handles the event.
After the dynamic type is created, the example gets a <xref:System.Reflection.MethodInfo> for the finished method and uses it to create a delegate instance. This instance is passed to the <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo.AddEventHandler*> method to hook up the event. The program then pauses to allow the event to be raised.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System.Reflection/EventInfo/AddEventHandler/source.cs"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Reflection/EventInfo/AddEventHandler/source.vb":::

Docs.exception: The event does not have a public accessor.
The handler that was passed in cannot be used.
The caller does not have access permission to the member.

The parameter is and the event is not static.
-or-
The is not declared on the target.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Overridable ReadOnly Property AddMethod As MethodInfo
public: virtual property System::Reflection::MethodInfo ^ AddMethod { System::Reflection::MethodInfo ^ get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets the object for the method of the event, including non-public methods.

remarks.format: ## Remarks This property is the equivalent of calling the <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo.GetAddMethod*> with a value of `true`.

MemberSignature.Value: Public MustOverride ReadOnly Property Attributes As EventAttributes
public: abstract property System::Reflection::EventAttributes Attributes { System::Reflection::EventAttributes get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets the attributes for this event.
Docs.value: The read-only attributes for this event.

remarks.format: ## Remarks The attributes are returned in a 4-byte integer representing a bitmap of the attributes set for the event reflected by this instance.

MemberSignature.Value: .method public hidebysig virtual instance bool Equals(object obj) cil managed
Public Overrides Function Equals (obj As Object) As Boolean

Docs.param: An object to compare with this instance, or .
Docs.summary: Returns a value that indicates whether this instance is equal to a specified object.
Docs.returns: if equals the type and value of this instance; otherwise, .

MemberSignature.Value: Public Overridable ReadOnly Property EventHandlerType As Type
public: virtual property Type ^ EventHandlerType { Type ^ get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets the object of the underlying event-handler delegate associated with this event.
Docs.value: A read-only object representing the delegate event handler.

remarks.format:
## Examples The following example uses the <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo.EventHandlerType> property to discover the delegate type of an event and to display its parameter types.
The example defines a delegate named `MyDelegate` and an event named `ev` of type `MyDelegate`. The code in the `Main` method discovers the event signature by getting the delegate type of the event, getting the `Invoke` method of the delegate type, and then retrieving and displaying the parameters.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System.Reflection/EventInfo/EventHandlerType/eventarg.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Reflection/EventInfo/EventHandlerType/eventarg.vb" id="Snippet1":::

Docs.exception: The caller does not have the required permission.

Docs.summary: Returns the method used to add an event handler delegate to the event source.

MemberSignature.Value: .method public hidebysig instance class System.Reflection.MethodInfo GetAddMethod() cil managed

Docs.summary: Returns the method used to add an event handler delegate to the event source.
Docs.returns: A object representing the method used to add an event handler delegate to the event source.

remarks.format:
## Remarks `GetAddMethod` initializes and adds the event subscribe method. The `AddEventHandler` method is used to add an event-handler delegate to the invocation list of an event source.
## Examples Typically, the method has the following signature:
```csharp add_<EventName>(<EventHandlerType> handler) ```

MemberSignature.Value: .method public hidebysig newslot virtual instance class System.Reflection.MethodInfo GetAddMethod(bool nonPublic) cil managed
Public MustOverride Function GetAddMethod (nonPublic As Boolean) As MethodInfo

Docs.param: if non-public methods can be returned; otherwise, .
Docs.summary: When overridden in a derived class, retrieves the object for the method of the event, specifying whether to return non-public methods.
Docs.returns: A object representing the method used to add an event handler delegate to the event source.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The `GetAddMethod` initializes and adds the event subscribe method as a Boolean value. The `AddEventHandler` method is used to add an event-handler delegate to the invocation list of an event source.
## Examples Typically, the method has the following signature:
```csharp add_<EventName>(<EventHandlerType> handler) ```

Docs.exception: is , the method used to add an event handler delegate is non-public, and the caller does not have permission to reflect on non-public methods.

MemberSignature.Value: .method public hidebysig virtual instance int32 GetHashCode() cil managed
Public Overrides Function GetHashCode () As Integer

Docs.summary: Returns the hash code for this instance.
Returns the methods that were associated with an event in MSIL using the directive.

MemberSignature.Value: .method public hidebysig instance class System.Reflection.MethodInfo[] GetOtherMethods() cil managed

Docs.summary: Returns the public methods that have been associated with an event in metadata using the directive.
Docs.returns: An array representing the public methods that have been associated with the event in metadata by using the directive. If there are no such public methods, an empty array is returned.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The metadata for an event can associate four kinds of methods with the event:
- The `.addon` directive specifies the method used to add event handlers. Use the <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo.GetAddMethod*> method to retrieve an <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo> for that method.
- The `.removeon` directive specifies the method used to detach event handlers. Use the <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo.GetRemoveMethod*> method to retrieve an <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo> for that method.
- The `.fire` directive specifies the method used to raise the event. Use the <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo.GetRaiseMethod*> method to retrieve an <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo> for that method.
- The `.other` directive specifies any other methods associated with the event. Use the <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo.GetOtherMethods*> method to retrieve an array of <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo> objects for those methods.
The methods associated with an event using the `.other` directive have no special significance to the runtime. The C# and Visual Basic compilers do not use the `.other` directive.

MemberSignature.Value: .method public hidebysig newslot virtual instance class System.Reflection.MethodInfo[] GetOtherMethods(bool nonPublic) cil managed
Public Overridable Function GetOtherMethods (nonPublic As Boolean) As MethodInfo()
abstract member GetOtherMethods : bool -> System.Reflection.MethodInfo[] override this.GetOtherMethods : bool -> System.Reflection.MethodInfo[]

Docs.summary: Returns the methods that have been associated with the event in metadata using the directive, specifying whether to include non-public methods.
Docs.returns: An array representing methods that have been associated with an event in metadata by using the directive. If there are no methods matching the specification, an empty array is returned.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The metadata for an event can associate four kinds of methods with the event:
- The `.addon` directive specifies the method used to add event handlers. Use the <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo.GetAddMethod*> method to retrieve an <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo> for that method.
- The `.removeon` directive specifies the method used to detach event handlers. Use the <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo.GetRemoveMethod*> method to retrieve an <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo> for this method.
- The `.fire` directive specifies the method used to raise the event. Use the <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo.GetRaiseMethod*> method to retrieve an <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo> for this method.
- The `.other` directive specifies any other methods associated with the event. Use the <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo.GetOtherMethods*> method to retrieve an array of <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo> objects for those methods.
The methods associated with an event using the `.other` directive have no special significance to the runtime. The C# and Visual Basic compilers do not use the `.other` directive.

Docs.summary: Returns the method that is called when the event is raised.

MemberSignature.Value: .method public hidebysig instance class System.Reflection.MethodInfo GetRaiseMethod() cil managed

Docs.summary: Returns the method that is called when the event is raised.
Docs.returns: The method that is called when the event is raised.

remarks.format: ## Remarks This method usually returns `null` for events declared with the C# `event` keyword or the Visual Basic `Event` keyword. This is because the C# and Visual Basic compilers do not generate such a method by default.

MemberSignature.Value: .method public hidebysig newslot virtual instance class System.Reflection.MethodInfo GetRaiseMethod(bool nonPublic) cil managed
Public MustOverride Function GetRaiseMethod (nonPublic As Boolean) As MethodInfo

Docs.param: if non-public methods can be returned; otherwise, .
Docs.summary: When overridden in a derived class, returns the method that is called when the event is raised, specifying whether to return non-public methods.
Docs.returns: A object that was called when the event was raised.

remarks.format: ## Remarks This method usually returns `null` for events declared with the C# `event` keyword or the Visual Basic `Event` keyword. This is because the C# and Visual Basic compilers do not generate such a method by default.

Docs.exception: is , the method used to add an event handler delegate is non-public, and the caller does not have permission to reflect on non-public methods.

Docs.summary: Returns the method used to remove an event handler delegate from the event source.

MemberSignature.Value: .method public hidebysig instance class System.Reflection.MethodInfo GetRemoveMethod() cil managed

Docs.summary: Returns the method used to remove an event handler delegate from the event source.
Docs.returns: A object representing the method used to remove an event handler delegate from the event source.

remarks.format:
## Examples Typically, the method has the following signature:
```csharp remove_<EventName>(<EventHandlerType> handler) ```

MemberSignature.Value: .method public hidebysig newslot virtual instance class System.Reflection.MethodInfo GetRemoveMethod(bool nonPublic) cil managed
Public MustOverride Function GetRemoveMethod (nonPublic As Boolean) As MethodInfo

Docs.param: if non-public methods can be returned; otherwise, .
Docs.summary: When overridden in a derived class, retrieves the object for removing a method of the event, specifying whether to return non-public methods.
Docs.returns: A object representing the method used to remove an event handler delegate from the event source.

remarks.format:
## Examples Typically, the method has the following signature:
```csharp remove_<EventName>(<EventHandlerType> handler) ```

Docs.exception: is , the method used to add an event handler delegate is non-public, and the caller does not have permission to reflect on non-public methods.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Overridable ReadOnly Property IsMulticast As Boolean
public: virtual property bool IsMulticast { bool get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the event is multicast.
Docs.value: if the delegate is an instance of a multicast delegate; otherwise, .
Docs.exception: The caller does not have the required permission.

MemberSignature.Value: Public ReadOnly Property IsSpecialName As Boolean
public: property bool IsSpecialName { bool get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the has a name with a special meaning.
Docs.value: if this event has a special name; otherwise, .

remarks.format: ## Remarks This property determines whether the event's name has a special meaning. Names that begin with or contain an underscore character (_), property accessors, and operator overloading methods are examples of names that might require special treatment by some compilers.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Overrides ReadOnly Property MemberType As MemberTypes
public: virtual property System::Reflection::MemberTypes MemberType { System::Reflection::MemberTypes get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets a value indicating that this member is an event.
Docs.value: A value indicating that this member is an event.

remarks.format: ## Remarks This property overrides <xref:System.Reflection.MemberInfo.MemberType*>. Therefore, when you examine a set of <xref:System.Reflection.MemberInfo> objects - for example, the array returned by <xref:System.Type.GetMembers*> - the <xref:System.Reflection.MemberInfo.MemberType> property returns <xref:System.Reflection.MemberTypes.Event?displayProperty=nameWithType> only when a given member is an event.

Docs.summary: Indicates whether two objects are equal.

Docs.summary: Indicates whether two objects are not equal.
Docs.returns: if is not equal to ; otherwise, .

MemberSignature.Value: Public Overridable ReadOnly Property RaiseMethod As MethodInfo
public: virtual property System::Reflection::MethodInfo ^ RaiseMethod { System::Reflection::MethodInfo ^ get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets the method that is called when the event is raised, including non-public methods.
Docs.value: The method that is called when the event is raised.

remarks.format: ## Remarks This property is the equivalent of calling the <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo.GetRaiseMethod*> with a value of `true`.

MemberSignature.Value: public virtual void RemoveEventHandler (object? target, Delegate? handler);
.method public hidebysig newslot virtual instance void RemoveEventHandler(object target, class System.Delegate handler) cil managed
Public Overridable Sub RemoveEventHandler (target As Object, handler As Delegate)
abstract member RemoveEventHandler : obj * Delegate -> unit override this.RemoveEventHandler : obj * Delegate -> unit
public: virtual void RemoveEventHandler(System::Object ^ target, Delegate ^ handler);
public virtual void RemoveEventHandler (object target, Delegate handler);

Docs.param: The delegate to be disassociated from the events raised by target.
Docs.summary: Removes an event handler from an event source.

remarks.format:
## Remarks This method attempts to remove the delegate that may synchronize this event on the target object.
When an event is raised by target, the method or methods encapsulated by `handler` will no longer be invoked.

Docs.exception: The event does not have a public accessor.
The handler that was passed in cannot be used.

The parameter is and the event is not static.
-or-
The is not declared on the target.
The caller does not have access permission to the member.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Overridable ReadOnly Property RemoveMethod As MethodInfo
public: virtual property System::Reflection::MethodInfo ^ RemoveMethod { System::Reflection::MethodInfo ^ get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets the object for removing a method of the event, including non-public methods.
Docs.value: The object for removing a method of the event.

remarks.format: ## Remarks This property is the equivalent of calling the <xref:System.Reflection.EventInfo.GetRemoveMethod*> with a value of `true`.
